steps.title: Identify the logical structure of the passage
steps.content: The passage establishes a paradox: astronomers know roughly 200 quasars formed in the universe's first billion years, yet conditions were "believed to have been ill suited" to quasar formation that early. The phrase "which suggests that" requires a conclusion that follows logically from this contradiction between what we observe and what our theory predicts.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘quasars that formed in the early universe are likely not as luminous as those that formed later.’: Luminosity is not mentioned in the passage and does not follow from the paradox about formation conditions. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘3C 273 is thought to have formed less than a billion years after the beginnings of the universe.’: This restates a detail about one specific quasar rather than drawing any conclusion from the paradox. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘3C 273 is likely less massive than quasars that formed more than a billion years after the beginnings of the universe.’: Mass comparisons are not supported by anything in the passage; this introduces unrelated speculation. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘some aspect of the scientific understanding of quasar formation or the early universe may be incomplete.’: When observed facts contradict theoretical predictions, the logical conclusion is that the theory is incomplete — exactly what the paradox implies. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
